summary: High-risk NDA from a Cayman Islands investment firm. Foreign jurisdiction significantly increases dispute resolution cost. Perpetual confidentiality obligation with no sunset clause. Missing No License Grant and Return/Destruction provisions. Asymmetric remedies favour counterparty exclusively.

playbook_results.deviation: Overly broad — includes all oral communications without requirement for written confirmation within 30 days. Creates unlimited scope of obligation.
'Best efforts' standard used instead of playbook-required 'same degree of care as own confidential information, no less than reasonable care'.
Perpetual obligation — no sunset clause. Playbook maximum is 5 years. Non-negotiable term is a red flag for any NDA.
Compelled disclosure provisions adequate. Prior written notice to disclosing party required.
Clause entirely absent. No obligation to return or destroy confidential information upon termination. Critical gap.
Receiving party (us) waives right to injunctive relief. Disclosing party retains full equitable remedies. Highly asymmetric.
Cayman Islands law and courts. Non-English governing law. Dispute resolution cost prohibitive for standard NDA.
One-way NDA accurately reflects the arrangement. No misrepresentation of scope.
Standard four exclusions present. Minor: independently developed information requires 'documented' proof — stricter than playbook.
No explicit no-license clause. Creates ambiguity around implied licence to confidential information shared.

critical_flags: CL-006: Asymmetric remedies — we waive injunctive relief
recommended_redlines: CL-003: Insert 'Term shall be 3 years from Effective Date' — remove perpetual obligation
CL-005: Insert standard return/destruction clause with 10-day certification requirement
CL-006: Remove waiver of injunctive relief for receiving party — must be mutual
CL-007: Negotiate to English law and courts (England & Wales preferred, New York acceptable)
CL-010: Insert 'Nothing herein shall be construed as granting any licence or right in any Confidential Information'
